pois.name: Icy Strait Point Dock
pois.notes: Tlingit-owned port complex. Ships dock directly at the restored cannery.

pois.name: ZipRider
pois.notes: World's largest zipline — 5,330 feet long, reaching 60mph. Gondola ride up, zip down.

pois.name: Whale Watching Tours
pois.notes: Point Adolphus is Alaska's premier humpback feeding ground in summer.

pois.name: Huna Tribal House
pois.notes: Tlingit clan house with cultural presentations, dance performances, and art.

pois.name: Historic Cannery Museum
pois.notes: Restored 1912 salmon cannery with museum exhibits about the Tlingit fishing heritage.

pois.name: Icy Strait Point Beach
pois.notes: Wilderness beach for tide-pooling and beachcombing.

pois.name: Spasski River Trail
pois.notes: Easy forest trail through old-growth Tongass National Forest.

pois.name: Point Adolphus
pois.notes: Alaska's premier humpback whale viewing area during summer months.

pois.name: Hoonah (town)
pois.notes: Tlingit village adjacent to the port with local shops and restaurants.

pois.name: Huna Heritage Foundation
pois.notes: Tlingit cultural center with exhibits, storytelling, and Native art.
